evals.prompt: Design a Grant Application doctype for an NGO. It needs: applicant org name, requested amount, program (linked to a Program doctype), submission date, status. Multi-stage approval: Program Officer reviews, then Grant Manager approves. Amounts over 50 lakhs escalate to CEO. Give me the full spec.
evals.expected_output: Complete spec with all 5 deliverables: (1) DocType schema with all fields including status Select field with all states, (2) form layout with section/column breaks, (3) list view config, (4) role permissions table, (5) workflow JSON with conditional escalation transition for amount > 5000000. Golden Rules applied: status field is read_only, workflow uses apply_workflow not db_set, states named in plain language.
assertions: DocType schema includes all requested fields with correct fieldtypes
Status field is Select type with all workflow states listed as options
Workflow JSON includes conditional transition with amount condition
Form layout includes Section Breaks and Column Breaks
Permissions table covers all relevant roles
Workflow transitions use plain language action names
Spec includes list view column recommendations
Does NOT use db_set for status updates

evals.prompt: I need a child table DocType called 'Grant Milestone' that lives inside a Grant Application. Fields: milestone title, due date, completion date, amount (Currency), status (Pending/Completed/Delayed). The parent Grant Application should show total milestone amount as a computed field.
evals.expected_output: Spec for child table DocType with istable:1 flag, correct fieldnames, parent link field. Guidance on embedding via Table fieldtype in parent DocType. Note about fetch_from or formula field for computing total milestone amount in parent. Should NOT include workflow on child table (child tables rarely have their own workflows).
assertions: All requested fields present with correct fieldtypes
Instructions for adding Table field to parent Grant Application DocType
Addresses total amount computation (formula field or controller logic)
Does not add unnecessary workflow to child table

evals.prompt: Our sidebar is blank for the Grant Manager role in v16. They can see the workspace tab but no items in the left panel. What's the most likely cause and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Systematic triage starting with most common cause (Bug #1 case-sensitivity in app_data_map). Should cover: (1) console check for frappe.boot.app_data_map casing, (2) role permissions on at least one DocType, (3) Workspace Roles table, (4) Desktop Icon parent+child pair existence, (5) Allow Modules on user profile. Should reference the verification checklist.
assertions: Provides browser console command to diagnose
Mentions role permissions on DocType (not just workspace)
Mentions Desktop Icon connector (parent + child pair)
Provides verification checklist or step-by-step triage

evals.prompt: Build a Frappe Page dashboard for mGrant showing: total grants disbursed (₹2.3 Cr), active grantees (1,247), pending approvals (18), and overdue reports (5). Include a bar chart showing disbursements by state and a table of recent grants with status.
evals.expected_output: Complete Frappe Page HTML with: KPI row using Indian formatting and short forms (₹2.3 Cr, 1.2K etc), mock data banner, filter strip with date+entity+status+search, bar chart using ColorBrewer palette, status chips (not plain text), table with right-aligned numbers, count-up animation, skeleton loading, elevation-1 shadows with hover lift, page structure matching the template (tabs→filters→KPIs→chart→table).

assertions.text: Uses Intl.NumberFormat('en-IN') or shortIN() function for number formatting
KPI cards have icon, number, label, and delta/growth indicator
All status fields rendered as .chip elements with color classes, not plain text
Mock data banner present with diagonal stripe CSS pattern
Filter strip includes date range, entity, status, search, and reset button
Chart uses a ColorBrewer palette with palette name in comment
frappe.call() used for API calls, not fetch() directly
Cards use box-shadow elevation-1 and hover lift (translateY)
Background is #F9FAFB (--gray-50), card surfaces are #FFFFFF
Page structure follows: tab bar → filter strip → KPI row → chart → table order

evals.prompt: Create an India state-level heatmap for a Frappe dashboard showing number of NGO partners per state. The map should allow drilling into districts when a state is clicked.
evals.expected_output: D3 + TopoJSON India map with fitBounds() called so map fills container, state-level choropleth with ColorBrewer YlOrRd palette, click-to-drill-down to district view, 'Back to India' button, Indian number formatting in tooltips, no tiny map in big whitespace.

assertions.text: projection.fitSize() or projection.fitExtent() called to fill container
ColorBrewer YlOrRd or Blues palette used for choropleth
'Back to India' button present and hidden at top level
Click handler on state paths triggers drill-down function
Tooltips show formatted numbers using Indian formatting
D3 and TopoJSON loaded from CDN

evals.prompt: I need a KPI summary card component for a Frappe ERPNext dashboard showing 'Active Mobile Health Units' = 42, up 3 from last month. Use the design system.
evals.expected_output: KPI card with 40x40 tinted icon, number '42' at 28px weight-700, label 'Active Mobile Health Units', delta '↑ 3 vs last month' in green, elevation-1 shadow, hover lift, count-up animation on load, tooltip showing full number.

assertions.text: Icon wrapper is 40x40px with rounded corners and semantic tinted background
Number displayed at 28px font-size with font-weight 700
Growth delta shows directional arrow (↑) with green color for positive
Delta includes comparison context text (vs last month / vs last FY etc)
CSS includes translateY hover animation
Count-up JS animation present for number
title attribute or tooltip shows full unformatted/full-format number
